name: Titanic
description: The RMS Titanic — its construction, sinking, rescue, wreck discovery, and the 1997 film

questions.q: Who was the captain of the RMS Titanic on its maiden voyage?

source.notes: Went down with the ship; 62 years old.

questions.q: From which English port did the Titanic depart on its maiden voyage?

source.notes: Departed April 10, 1912; also stopped at Cherbourg and Queenstown.

questions.q: What was the Titanic's intended destination?

source.notes: The Carpathia ultimately delivered survivors there on April 18.

questions.q: Who directed the 1997 film Titanic, which tied the all-time Academy Awards record with 11 wins?

source.notes: The film held the all-time box office record until Avatar (2009).

questions.q: Who played Jack Dawson in the 1997 film Titanic?

source.notes: Paired opposite Kate Winslet as Rose.

questions.q: In what year did the Titanic sink?

source.notes: Sank in the early hours of April 15 after striking an iceberg April 14.

questions.q: What physically struck the Titanic, ultimately causing her to sink?

source.notes: At approximately 11:40 pm on April 14, 1912.

questions.q: Which Belfast shipbuilder constructed the Titanic?

source.notes: Built for White Star Line in Belfast, Northern Ireland.

questions.q: Which shipping company operated the Titanic?

source.notes: Chairman J. Bruce Ismay was aboard and survived.

questions.q: Which ship arrived to rescue Titanic survivors from the lifeboats?

source.notes: Cunard liner; arrived on scene around 4:00 am April 15.

questions.q: Roughly how many people died in the Titanic disaster?

source.notes: Of roughly 2,224 passengers and crew; around 710 survived.

questions.q: What were the names of the Titanic's two sister ships?

source.notes: All three were Olympic-class liners; Britannic was sunk by a mine in 1916.

questions.q: Which nearby ship notoriously failed to respond to the Titanic's distress calls?

source.notes: Her captain Stanley Lord was heavily criticized by inquiries.

questions.q: In what year was the Titanic's wreck discovered on the Atlantic seafloor?

source.notes: Joint US-French expedition; secretly funded by the US Navy.

questions.q: Which oceanographer led the 1985 expedition that discovered the Titanic wreck?

source.notes: Used the Argo towed submersible camera system.

questions.q: How many lifeboats did the Titanic carry — famously too few for the 2,200+ on board?

source.notes: Complied with outdated 1894 regulations; total capacity ~1,178.

questions.q: At what time (ship's time) did the Titanic strike the iceberg on April 14, 1912?

source.notes: The ship sank about 2 hours 40 minutes later.

questions.q: Who composed the Oscar-winning score for the 1997 Titanic?

source.notes: Also co-wrote 'My Heart Will Go On' with Will Jennings.

questions.q: Approximately how deep below the surface does the Titanic wreck lie?

source.notes: About 370 nautical miles south-southeast of Newfoundland.

questions.q: Who was the Titanic's chief designer, who perished with the ship?

source.notes: Managing director of Harland and Wolff's design department.

questions.q: What was the name of the experimental submersible that imploded while descending to the Titanic wreck in June 2023?

source.notes: Operated by OceanGate; killed all five on board.

questions.q: Who was the commander of the Carpathia who led the rescue of Titanic survivors?

source.notes: Awarded a silver cup and gold medal by the survivors.
